validation.rationale: RCA root cause + 2-step fix direction approved by user 2026-05-24 (transcript). Scope is bug-fix (orphaned RED scaffold from closed phase-1-first-workload feature; contract is pinned by ADR-0025 step 5 + ADR-0029 amendment; no DISCUSS/DESIGN/DISTILL wave artefacts needed beyond the existing ADRs). Architect/reviewer dispatch skipped per /nw-bugfix minimal-2-step convention — see rca.md.

steps.name: Regression test (RED) — pin boot-time node_health write absent today
steps.criteria:
AC for the regression contract:
- New integration test file `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/node_health_writer_runs_at_boot.rs` under the existing `tests/integration.rs` `#![cfg(feature = "integration-tests")]` entrypoint mod tree (per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Integration vs unit gating" → "Mechanics").
- Test boots `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` against a `SimObservationStore` (and `SimDriver` per the existing test pattern) using the existing test helpers — must use the SAME boot path the user-facing CLI invokes (via `run_server` → `run_server_with_obs_and_driver`), not a hand-rolled construction skipping the call site under audit.
- After the server is up, the test reads `obs.node_health_rows().await?` and asserts:
  - Exactly one row is returned (`.len() == 1`).
  - The row's `node_id` matches the configured override (test passes `NodeConfig.id_override = Some("local")`).
  - The row's `region` matches the configured value.
  - The row's `last_heartbeat: LogicalTimestamp` is non-default (LogicalTimestamp::default or epoch is not the same as a freshly-sampled tick).
- DELETE the existing RED-scaffold pin `write_node_health_row_is_red_scaffold_until_slice_4_green` from `crates/overdrive-worker/src/node_health.rs::tests` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "RED scaffolds and intentionally-failing commits" — "When slice 4 GREEN lands, this test is REMOVED." The new boot-time regression test (above) replaces it as the structural contract.
- The test MUST FAIL against current `origin/main` (the writer is unwired): verify by running `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(node_health_writer_runs_at_boot)'` before the deletion lands — fail with `assertion left: 0, right: 1` (zero rows in the store).
- The deletion of the should_panic pin must NOT be done by replacing the body with `Ok(())` or a neutral stub (per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Downstream fallout on pre-existing tests"); the file is fully deleted from `mod tests`.
- Quality gates after this step:
  - `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --features integration-tests` clean.
  - `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests --no-deps -- -D warnings` clean.
  - `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --features integration-tests -E 'test(node_health_writer_runs_at_boot)'` FAILS with the expected assertion (NOT a compile error, NOT a panic from the unrelated scaffold).
  - `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run -p overdrive-worker` clean (the should_panic pin is gone; no other test in that crate should break).

Scope OUT (defer to 01-02):
- Implementing `write_node_health_row` body.
- Adding `start_local_node` helper.
- Wiring into `run_server_with_obs_and_driver`.
- Typed `#[from]` error variant on `ControlPlaneError`.
- Lima `GET /v1/nodes` test.

Scope OUT (any wave):
- A periodic heartbeat reconciler. ADR-0025 frames the writer as one-shot at boot; the future periodic-write reconciler is out-of-scope per the rca.md "What this fix is NOT" section.
- A worker-startup framework with extension points. The `start_local_node` helper (added in 01-02) is single-purpose today.
steps.description: Step 01-01 of the 2-step bug-fix roadmap. Establishes the regression contract: a test that fails today (because the writer is unwired) and will pass after 01-02 lands the wiring + implementation. Also removes the now-redundant `#[should_panic]` RED-scaffold pin in node_health.rs::tests — the new boot-time regression IS the contract, replacing the structural defence the should_panic pin used to provide. After this step, the suite is RED on exactly one test (the new regression test) and clean elsewhere; this is the GREEN-target state for step 01-02.

steps.name: Implementation (GREEN) — writer body + boot-path wiring + typed error + tests pass
steps.criteria:
AC for the GREEN landing — close 01-01's RED:
- (a) Implement `write_node_health_row` body per ADR-0025 step 5 + ADR-0029 worker-startup amendment:
  - Signature gains `clock: &Arc<dyn Clock>` parameter (the existing scaffold takes only `obs` + `config`). All call sites (currently none in production; only the deleted test pin) are updated atomically. NOT optional — every Clock dependency at construction time per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Port-trait dependencies".
  - Resolve `NodeId`: `config.id_override.as_ref().map(|s| s.parse::<NodeId>()).transpose().map_err(|e| NodeHealthWriteError::IdResolve(e.to_string()))?` fallback to `NodeId::from_hostname()` (or equivalent — verify exact API during PREPARE; if missing, surface as a blocker, do NOT invent).
  - Build the `NodeHealthRowV1 { node_id, region: config.region.clone(), last_heartbeat: <Clock-derived LogicalTimestamp> }` via the public alias `NodeHealthRow` per `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs:362` (UI-02 alias-to-payload — construct the struct directly with literal syntax).
  - Write via `obs.write(ObservationRow::NodeHealth(row)).await.map_err(|e| NodeHealthWriteError::Write(e.to_string()))?`.
- (b) New `pub async fn overdrive_worker::start_local_node(obs: &Arc<dyn ObservationStore>, config: &NodeConfig, clock: &Arc<dyn Clock>) -> Result<(), NodeHealthWriteError>` helper exported from `crates/overdrive-worker/src/lib.rs`. Single-purpose now (writes the node_health row); the helper exists to mark the ADR-0029 worker-startup contract boundary without speculative scaffolding.
- (c) Wire `overdrive_worker::start_local_node(&obs, &node_config, &clock).await.map_err(error::ControlPlaneError::from)?` into `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`, AFTER the `create_workloads_slice_with_controllers` call (~line 794) and BEFORE the rustls crypto provider install (~line 805). This places the write per ADR-0025 step 5 ordering: ObservationStore wired, before TLS material, before listener bind.
- (d) Typed error: add `#[error(transparent)] NodeHealthWrite(#[from] overdrive_worker::node_health::NodeHealthWriteError)` (or equivalent variant + #[from] impl) to `error::ControlPlaneError` in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/error.rs`. ABSOLUTELY DO NOT use `ControlPlaneError::internal("context", e)` per `.claude/rules/development.md` § "Never flatten a typed error to `Internal(String)` at a composition boundary."
- (e) `ServerConfig` (in `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs`) gains an additive `node: NodeConfig` field (or its three constituents `node_id_override`, `node_region`, `node_capacity` — pick the cleaner shape during PREPARE). Default values populate the call-path in tests/dev; production via CLI threading. If `NodeConfig` lives in `overdrive-worker` (it does — `crates/overdrive-worker/src/node_health.rs:55`), re-export from worker's `lib.rs` and consume from control-plane.
- (f) Unit test under `crates/overdrive-worker/src/node_health.rs` `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`: against `SimObservationStore`, call `write_node_health_row(&obs, &config, &clock)` with a `SimClock` set to a known tick, then `obs.node_health_rows().await?` returns one row whose `last_heartbeat` matches the SimClock's logical time. NodeId comes from override path AND the hostname-fallback path covered in TWO separate test cases (split — do NOT collapse into one test asserting two cases).
- (g) Lima integration test under `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/node_health_writer_runs_at_boot.rs` (the file added in 01-01) — EXTEND the file with a second test scoped to the operator-visible KPI: boot the test server, hit `GET /v1/nodes` via the test HTTP client (use the existing pattern from `tests/integration/backend_discovery_bridge/test_server.rs`), assert the response body deserialises to a single-element array whose row matches the configured node. Both tests in this file run under `--features integration-tests`.
- (h) `NodeHealthRowEnvelope` golden-bytes fixture: verify presence per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Property-based testing → Archive schema-evolution roundtrip". Path `crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/node_health_row.rs` (or equivalent under the schema_evolution entrypoint). If MISSING, add it in-scope (this is blocking, not deferrable, per project rules). If PRESENT, leave untouched — the existing fixture is the structural defence and must NOT be re-pinned per § version-bump procedure step 5.
- (i) Update any existing test that asserted `GET /v1/nodes` returns `[]` on fresh boot — those tests will break because the boot-time write now lands one row. Search via `grep -r 'node_health_rows\|/v1/nodes' crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/`. Update expected values; do NOT add ignore attributes.

Quality gates after this step:
- `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo check --workspace --features integration-tests` clean.
- `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --features integration-tests --no-deps -- -D warnings` clean. Fix any clippy issues in-scope (per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer`) — do NOT defer to follow-up.
- `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests` clean workspace-wide.
- `cargo test --doc --workspace` clean (touch any doctest that references the old signature).
- The 01-01 regression test (`boot_writes_exactly_one_node_health_row_to_observation_store`) PASSES with `assertion left: 1, right: 1`.
- The new Lima `GET /v1/nodes` test PASSES with single-element array.
- `cargo run -p xtask -- dst-lint` exits 0 (the writer + helper live in `adapter-host`-class `overdrive-worker`, so dst-lint does not scan them, but `run_server_with_obs_and_driver` lives in `overdrive-control-plane` — verify the call site doesn't introduce banned APIs).
- `git status` shows the should_panic pin deletion from 01-01 is still in place; the implementation does NOT re-add a panic-bodied scaffold or a placeholder fixture test.

Files touched (production):
- `crates/overdrive-worker/src/node_health.rs` (body + signature + unit tests)
- `crates/overdrive-worker/src/lib.rs` (export `start_local_node`)
- `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/lib.rs` (call site in `run_server_with_obs_and_driver`; `ServerConfig` additive field)
- `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/error.rs` (typed `#[from]` variant)
- Possibly `crates/overdrive-cli/src/...` (if `ServerConfig` is constructed there with the new field)

Files touched (tests):
- `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/integration/node_health_writer_runs_at_boot.rs` (extend with GET /v1/nodes test)
- Possibly `crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/node_health_row.rs` (if fixture missing)
- Possibly existing tests asserting `GET /v1/nodes` returns []

KPI: ADR-0025 step 5 contract is observable — single-node boot writes one row; operator GET /v1/nodes returns it.

Disproves:
- "The boot path can stay incomplete vs ADR-0025 without operator-visible consequence." → No, GET /v1/nodes returns [] today on a healthy single-node deployment; this fix proves the contract gap was real and closes it.
steps.description: Step 01-02 of the 2-step bug-fix roadmap. Implements the writer body per ADR-0025 step 5, wires it into the boot path via the new `overdrive_worker::start_local_node` ADR-0029 contract-boundary helper, and adds the typed `#[from]` error variant on `ControlPlaneError`. Closes the 01-01 regression RED. Adds unit tests (SimObservationStore round-trip with override path + hostname fallback path) and extends the Lima integration test with the operator-visible `GET /v1/nodes` KPI. Verifies the NodeHealthRowEnvelope schema-evolution fixture exists (adds it in-scope if not). Updates any existing test that depended on `GET /v1/nodes` returning [] on fresh boot.
